channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: "Nightmare": Trump Claims China Interfered In 2020 US Polls, Beijing Responds

media:content.media:description: Trump addressed US voters in televised prime-time address from the White House.

item.description: More than 60 lawsuits by Trump and his allies produced no ruling establishing fraud capable of changing the outcome of the 2020 election, while recounts, audits, and his own Justice Department found none.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1128737/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>More than 60 lawsuits by Trump and his allies produced no ruling establishing fraud capable of changing the outcome of the 2020 election, while recounts, audits, and his own Justice Department found none.

item.title: US-Iran War News LIVE: 7 Killed As US Launches 6th Consecutive Night Of Strikes Against Iran
item.description: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.

item.title: Team Trump Alters Rule To Deny Green Cards To Immigrants Using Public Benefits

media:content.media:description: There were 22.8 million noncitizens living in the U.S. in 2023, according to the Census Bureau.

item.description: The Trump administration is reviving a rule that could deny green cards to immigrants who use public benefits that could include food stamps, Medicaid, housing vouchers and others.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration is reviving a rule that could deny green cards to immigrants who use public benefits that could include food stamps, Medicaid, housing vouchers and others.

item.title: Firefighters Give Oxygen To Pigeon After It Suffers Smoke Inhalation From Car Fire In US

media:content.media:description: The background of the video showed a chaotic scene with flashing lights and a fire truck.

item.description: Oakland firefighters assisted a pigeon suffering smoke inhalation from a car fire by administering oxygen before it flew away.
item.content:encoded: Oakland firefighters assisted a pigeon suffering smoke inhalation from a car fire by administering oxygen before it flew away.

item.title: Nitish Kaushal, Member Of Jaggu Bhagwanpuria Gang, Arrested In US

media:content.media:description: Indian-origin gangster Nitish Kaushal has been arrested in Vermont.

item.description: Indian-origin gangster Nitish Kaushal's arrest comes two days after FBI put him to its "wanted" list.
item.content:encoded: Indian-origin gangster Nitish Kaushal's arrest comes two days after FBI put him to its "wanted" list.

item.title: US Attacks Iran In Tit-For-Tat Strikes, Hits Airport, Railway Station, Bridges

media:content.media:description: According to Iranian media, two people had died and four were injured in the attack.

item.description: US strikes overnight hit an airport and two bridges in southern Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state media said Friday.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1128724/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>US strikes overnight hit an airport and two bridges in southern Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state media said Friday.

item.title: She Knew He Wasn't Real. One Woman's Romance Scam Story

media:content.media:description: Romance scams are on the rise across the United States.

item.description: She knew somewhere in the back of her mind that Donnie didn't exist. She also knew that before she hit the road again, in spite of all logic and months of warnings from everyone in her life, she'd send the money anyway.
item.content:encoded: She knew somewhere in the back of her mind that Donnie didn't exist. She also knew that before she hit the road again, in spite of all logic and months of warnings from everyone in her life, she'd send the money anyway.

item.title: US Announces Visa Curbs On Members Linked To "Far-Left Terrorist" Groups

media:content.media:description: Marco Rubio said leftist extremism draws its inspiration from a "hatred for civilization itself."

item.description: The United States on Thursday launched a scathing attack on left-wing extremists, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio calling for global cooperation to fight a "new wave of this old evil."
item.content:encoded: The United States on Thursday launched a scathing attack on left-wing extremists, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio calling for global cooperation to fight a "new wave of this old evil."

item.title: SpaceX Starship Rocket Aborts Before Liftoff In 13th Flight Test Attempt

media:content.media:description: The rocket's engines ignited but cut off shortly after, it said.

item.description: SpaceX's Starship rocket triggered a last-second abort before liftoff for its 13th flight test from Texas on Thursday, postponing the mission by at least 24 hours with the company studying what likely caused the automated scrub.
item.content:encoded: SpaceX's Starship rocket triggered a last-second abort before liftoff for its 13th flight test from Texas on Thursday, postponing the mission by at least 24 hours with the company studying what likely caused the automated scrub.

item.title: US Immigrant Agent In Maine Shooting Has History Of Violent Behavior: Family

media:content.media:description: The White House referred all questions about the shooting and Brouillette to ICE.

item.description: The Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer who shot a Colombian man in Maine this week is an Army veteran who has struggled with serious mental health issues since early childhood, close relatives told.
item.content:encoded: The Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer who shot a Colombian man in Maine this week is an Army veteran who has struggled with serious mental health issues since early childhood, close relatives told.

item.title: Philippines Condemns Chinese Media's Video Depicting Filipinos As Monkeys

media:content.media:description: The video portrayed a monkey dressed in Filipino attire.

item.description: China Daily's AI-generated video was posted on July 10, coinciding with Philippine events marking the 10th anniversary of a landmark arbitral ruling that invalidated China's sweeping claims in the South China Sea.
item.content:encoded: China Daily's AI-generated video was posted on July 10, coinciding with Philippine events marking the 10th anniversary of a landmark arbitral ruling that invalidated China's sweeping claims in the South China Sea.

item.title: Trump Media To Sell High Speed Access To Truth Social Posts

media:content.media:description: It said that the service, starting August 1, could benefit traders and news organizations.

item.description: Donald Trump's media company said Thursday it planned to sell faster access to market-moving social media posts by the US president.
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump's media company said Thursday it planned to sell faster access to market-moving social media posts by the US president.

item.title: US Forces Continue "Wave Of Strikes" Against Iran For 6th Consecutive Night

media:content.media:description: US forces on Thursday launched a fresh wave of military strikes against Iran.

item.description: According to the US Central Command (CENTCOM), the attack was aimed at further degrading Iran's military capabilities, as Tehran poses a threat to commercial vessels transiting through the Strait of Hormuz, which Washington claims.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1128773/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>According to the US Central Command (CENTCOM), the attack was aimed at further degrading Iran's military capabilities, as Tehran poses a threat to commercial vessels transiting through the Strait of Hormuz, which Washington claims.

item.title: Xi Jinping To Present China's AI Dream At Major Conference Today

media:content.media:description: Xi Jinping will speak at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in Shanghai on Friday.

item.description: President Xi Jinping presents China's vision for the development and regulation of artificial intelligence on Friday, at a major conference showcasing cutting-edge technology he hopes will soon rival that of the United States.
item.content:encoded: President Xi Jinping presents China's vision for the development and regulation of artificial intelligence on Friday, at a major conference showcasing cutting-edge technology he hopes will soon rival that of the United States.

item.title: "Crude And Arrogant": Brazil Hits Out At Marco Rubio Over Remarks On Lula

media:content.media:description: Brazil's Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira said Rubio's remarks were "unacceptable and offensive."

item.description: Brazil's Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira on Thursday accused his US counterpart Marco Rubio of "attacking" President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva in a "crude and arrogant" manner when commenting on a new tariff decision.
item.content:encoded: Brazil's Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira on Thursday accused his US counterpart Marco Rubio of "attacking" President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva in a "crude and arrogant" manner when commenting on a new tariff decision.

item.title: 'SAARC, BIMSTEC Don't Need To Be Rivals': Bangladesh At Delhi Meet

media:content.media:description: This isn't the first time Dhaka has brought SAARC up.

item.description: According to a statement by the Ministry of External Affairs, the meeting pulled together security advisers and heads of delegations from all six other member states, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka and Thailand.
item.content:encoded: According to a statement by the Ministry of External Affairs, the meeting pulled together security advisers and heads of delegations from all six other member states, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka and Thailand.

item.title: Trump Administration Tightens Visa Limits For Foreign Students And Journalists

media:content.media:description: Foreign journalists would be limited to stays of just 240 days, or around eight months

item.description: Under a change that could be implemented as soon as September, foreigners on student visas would be admitted for the length of their academic programme up to a maximum of four years.
item.content:encoded: Under a change that could be implemented as soon as September, foreigners on student visas would be admitted for the length of their academic programme up to a maximum of four years.

item.title: Trump To Accuse China Of Meddling In US Elections In Primetime Speech: Report

media:content.media:description: Trump has claimed that the 2020 presidential election was stolen because of fraud

item.description: Several of the country's top national security officials are expected to attend the speech. Those invited include the heads of the CIA, FBI, Office of the Director of National Intelligence, Department of Homeland Security and other senior officials.
item.content:encoded: Several of the country's top national security officials are expected to attend the speech. Those invited include the heads of the CIA, FBI, Office of the Director of National Intelligence, Department of Homeland Security and other senior officials.

item.title: EU Report Raises Rights Concerns As Pakistan Reaps Billions In Trade Benefits

media:content.media:description: Looking ahead, EU officials say Islamabad must show real accountability for rights violations

item.description: Pakistan pocketed roughly "732 million in EU tariff exemptions last year, but a new joint assessment from the European Commission and the EU's foreign policy arm paints a troubling picture of the human rights record.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan pocketed roughly "732 million in EU tariff exemptions last year, but a new joint assessment from the European Commission and the EU's foreign policy arm paints a troubling picture of the human rights record.

item.title: Troops In Hormuz Islands, Bombing Pickaxe Mountain: Trump's Big Iran War Plan

media:content.media:description: One of the biggest proposals under discussion is an operation to seize Kharg Island

item.description: Trump held a high-level meeting in the White House Situation Room on Tuesday night, where senior officials discussed several military options.
item.content:encoded: Trump held a high-level meeting in the White House Situation Room on Tuesday night, where senior officials discussed several military options.
